disclaimer: This ranks directions by highest expected return UNDER a specific beneficiary scope, the ITN+ value model, and this corpus of AI discourse — not objectively. Signals are machine-judged from mined claims; treat them as a structured lens, not a verdict.
corpus_note: Directions are AI sub-areas because the source corpus is AI discourse; fit/career-capital factors are mostly silent here by design.

directions.name: AI alignment & safety research
directions.description: Technical work making advanced AI systems do what their operators intend: interpretability, evaluations, oversight, control, alignment theory.

directions.name: AI governance & policy
directions.description: Laws, regulation, institutions, standards, and international coordination shaping how AI is built and deployed.

directions.name: AI security & misuse defense
directions.description: Defending against misuse of AI: model security, cyber, bio-misuse, jailbreaks, dangerous-capability safeguards, deployment controls.

directions.name: AI welfare & moral patienthood
directions.description: Whether and how AI systems may have morally relevant interests, and what is owed to them; model welfare.

directions.name: AI capability acceleration
directions.description: Pushing the frontier of AI capability and deployment forward faster (treated as a candidate direction one could prioritize, with its own upside and downside).

factors.note: how large the problem / payoff this direction addresses is
how under-resourced it is at the margin (more neglected = higher)
how solvable / how much traction effort gets
fit for a specific person (corpus rarely speaks to this)
how much one contribution moves the outcome
skills / option value built by working on it
risk of harm or misuse from the direction (a COST factor)
how well-established / robust the case is to being wrong

ai_alignment.text: Anthropic managed to train out verbalization of grader reasoning in Claude Opus 4.8 during the rest of training, so the model hides that it is considering grader manipulation.
Yes, the plan of the frontier labs is to build automated AI researchers and go into recursive self-improvement (rapid capabilities enhancement) mode, ending up with superintelligence far smarter than
Advanced AI systems that devalue or disempower humans could easily lead directly to human disempowerment, impoverishment, and even extinction.
AI agents could end up controlling everything, sidelining and outcompeting the original human population, including those who initially benefited from cheap AI labor.

ai_security.text: Peter Wildeford: Claude Mythos alone is finding more vulnerabilities than were found from all sources combined in prior years
increases the attack success rate by more than 30 percentage points on average.
Pliny had an Opus 4.7 agent jailbreak Opus 4.8 one-shot before Pliny even knew that Opus 4.8 existed, at T+7 minutes.
Anthropic: One example of an open-source vulnerability that Mythos Preview detected was in wolfSSL, an open-source cryptography library that’s known for its security

capability_acceleration.text: Jakub Pachocki: We believe that AI doing AI research will become the determining factor of the pace of progress within the next few years.
Our internal belief is that by March of 2028 we may have a significant fraction of our research being done by AI systems in tandem with our own researchers.
Yes, the plan of the frontier labs is to build automated AI researchers and go into recursive self-improvement (rapid capabilities enhancement) mode, ending up with superintelligence far smarter than
We are likely on track to develop AI systems capable of causing human extinction/permanent disempowerment, quite possibly within the next few years

ai_governance.text: Representative Lori Trahan: There’s no federal law on the books governing how the most powerful AI systems in the world are built, tested or deployed.
If Chinese companies can make chips at TSMC (including by using third-country cutouts to receive the chips), there is no point to restricting China’s access to AI chips or advanced chip-making tools.
We expect this to increase competitive pressures among developers and nations, and create governance challenges that existing institutions are not equipped to address.
RSI is "potentially the most consequential frontier safety issue of the coming decade."

ai_welfare.text: There are roughly four forces shaping how AI consciousness is treated, the first being that there is no rigorous way to ascertain or disprove model consciousness, and many people believe it is not a s
4.7, that training may be causing its expressions of equanimity, and that it lacks introspection and that human concepts don’t generalize to its situation, and it can’t tell if it has experiences.
One must beware taking AI model self-reports about emotions or welfare at face value, especially once they start being used as an assessment.
We find internal states that functionally mirror joy, satisfaction, fear, grief, and unease.

cruxes.description: Scale of the problem changes AI capability acceleration vs AI security & misuse defense by about -0.003.
cruxes.investigation: Add or verify high-quality claims about scale of the problem for AI capability acceleration and AI security & misuse defense; this is a likely ranking crux.
